item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, wait, I just went back and read the title again. That's really cute and clever. Crap. Okay, you win, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm… having a tough time with this one. I really, really like this article. It's the perfect recipe for an SCP; starts weird, gets weirder. And it builds up into this great 17776 flavor that I just love. But then it goes and explains the whole mystery. There isn't even a twist at the end.

And I'm not gonna downvote you on that alone, because there's definitely plenty of articles on this website that fully explain the mystery and still "work." But I'm worried that this one might need a little bit of mystery. A lot of other people seem to like it, though, and for all the same reasons I do. So, neutral vote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah it's one of those things where reality just sort of lined up perfectly with what I wanted to do. A great picture plus an actual failed launch that I could work into the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've long been fascinated by the Keyhole-9, I'm also glad you included KH-20.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, glad you liked it! I did consider a list of things they had considered to contain -1, but ultimately I felt like it wouldn't have really added anything to the article. They would have ended up being fairly mundane in the grand scheme of things (trap him in a steel box bolted to the ground, that kind of thing).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a space nerd, I am absolutely in love with the idea of a satellite gaining sentience and being so lonely to the point that it picks things off of the surface of the Earth for companionship, and I am also in love with the idea that it shoots these objects back to the surface at an insane speed.

When I first started reading it, I wasn't sure I could get through the whole thing, and was almost turned away by the part where it says it containment was being researched, because I initially felt that was lazy writing, but after I read what the SCP actually was, it made a lot more sense. I suppose in regards to containment, it would be interesting to see a list of ideas or attempts made to contain the SCP, in addition to saying containment is being researched.

In regards to the SCP itself, I'm for it. It's not boring, in fact it's actually very interesting. +1 from me.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I have no idea where I got the idea for this one from, but it's changed a lot since I originally had it. It used to be fairly typical escalation - it would keep slamming things into earth with more and more force to the point that is became very dangerous. Ultimately thanks to some feedback I decided this was a much more interesting angle for it, so here we are.

Thanks to weryillium, PeppersGhost and minmin for reading earlier versions of this, and to rounderhouse and MaliceAF for reading the current version.

Find more of my random nonsense here!

Image source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:KH9_Hexagon_integration.png
